      public static org.bukkit.entity.Entity getTargetEntity(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Deprecated.
      Get the target Entity a player is looking at
      Parameters:
      player - that is looking at the entity
      Returns:
      Entity that the player is looking At
      Since:
      1.0.0

    • randomPotionEffect

      public static org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType randomPotionEffect()
      Get's a Random Potion Effect Type
      Returns:
      Random Potion Effect Type

    • getNearbyChunks

      public static org.bukkit.Chunk[] getNearbyChunks(org.bukkit.Location location, int range) throws ExecutionException, InterruptedException, TimeoutException
      Retrieves an array of chunks near the specified location within the given range.
      Parameters:
      location - the central location from which nearby chunks are determined
      range - the distance in chunks to search for nearby chunks
      Returns:
      an array of chunks located within the specified range of the given location
      Throws:
      ExecutionException
      InterruptedException
      TimeoutException

    • getSoulPoints

      public static int getSoulPoints(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Get's the SoulPoints of a Currenly Selected Player
      Parameters:
      player - who is currently selected
      Returns:
      Integer of the SoulPoints of the player

    • itemToString

      public static String itemToString(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Converts and Item Into a string
      Parameters:
      item - that is being converted into a string
      Returns:
      string of the item in Yaml format

    • stringToItem

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack stringToItem(String data)
      Converts a String into an Item
      Parameters:
      data - that is being converted into an item, it's in standard yaml format
      Returns:
      ItemStack of the item that was converted from the string

    • locationToString

      public static String locationToString(org.bukkit.Location loc)
      Converts a location into a string, which is stored "x,y,z"
      Parameters:
      loc - Location that is being converted into a string
      Returns:
      String of the location in the format "world,x,y,z"

    • stringToLocations

      public static ArrayList<org.bukkit.Location> stringToLocations(String loc)
      Converts a string into a list of locations
      Parameters:
      loc - String that is being converted into a list of locations
      Returns:
      locations that were converted from the string

    • randomPositivePotionEffect

      public static org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType randomPositivePotionEffect()
      Get's a random Positive Potion Effect
      Returns:
      Positive Potion Effect

    • randomNegativePotionEffect

      public static org.bukkit.potion.PotionEffectType randomNegativePotionEffect()
      Gets a random Negative Potion Effect
      Returns:
      Negative Potion Effect

    • plush

      public static void plush(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.event.player.PlayerInteractEvent event, BaseBlock baseBlock)
      Plush Squish Animation
      Parameters:
      player - player who is squishing the plush
      event - player interactevent that was triggered to squish the plush
      baseBlock - baseblock that was squished

    • emptyItem

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack emptyItem(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Empty Item Constructor of a Base ItemStack
      Parameters:
      item - inherits item type from this but set's display name
      Returns:
      ItemStack of the item type but with the display name set to the empty item name

    • keygen

      public static org.bukkit.NamespacedKey keygen(String key)
      Creates a new Namespaced Key with a String "id"
      Parameters:
      key - String Key ID to generate NamespacedKey
      Returns:
      NamespacedKey of the key "id" from earlier

    • directionTo

      public static org.bukkit.util.Vector directionTo(org.bukkit.Location start, org.bukkit.Location end)
      Gets the direction between two locations to create a vector line
      Parameters:
      start - Start Location of the Vector Line
      end - End Location of the Vector Line
      Returns:
      Vector of the direction from start to end

    • dess

      public static net.kyori.adventure.text.Component dess(String minimessage)
      Creates a component using Mini Message with a given string without the long Minimessage.miniMessage().deserialize(String)
      Parameters:
      minimessage - String of Minimessage you want to construct the component
      Returns:
      Component Construction of the Minimessage String

    • getCachedPdcValue

      public static <T,Z> Z getCachedPdcValue(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, String key, org.bukkit.persistence.PersistentDataType<T,Z> type)
      Retrieves a cached value associated with a player's Persistent Data Container (PDC). If the value exists in the cache and is still valid, it is returned directly from the cache. Otherwise, the value is fetched from the PDC, stored in the cache, and then returned.
      Type Parameters:
      T - The primitive type the PersistentDataType maps from.
      Z - The object type the PersistentDataType maps to.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose PDC is being accessed.
      key - The key that identifies the specific value within the PDC.
      type - The data type of the value being fetched from the PDC.
      Returns:
      The value associated with the given key and type, or null if the key is not present in the PDC.

    • getMidpoint

      public static org.bukkit.Location getMidpoint(org.bukkit.Location loc1, org.bukkit.Location loc2)
      Gets midpoint between two locations
      Parameters:
      loc1 - Location one to get midpoint
      loc2 - Location two to get midpoint
      Returns:
      Midpoint Location between two locations

    • showEntityToPlayer

      public static void showEntityToPlayer(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.entity.Entity entity)
      Shows Entity to Player if that Entity was hidden using
      Parameters:
      player - player to show entity to
      entity - entity to show to player
      See Also:
      • hideEntityFromPlayer(Player, Entity)

    • pulseCircle

      public void pulseCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, double smallestRadius, int pulsingTime, int pulses, org.bukkit.Sound sound, Object options)
      Creates a Particle Pulse Circle, which is a circle that expands a couple times from a center point
      Parameters:
      center - Location of the center of the circle
      radius - Radius of the circle
      points - Number of points to use for the circle
      particle - Particle to use for the circle
      smallestRadius - Smallest radius to use for the circle
      pulsingTime - Time to pulse the circle
      pulses - how many pulses to do
      sound - Sound to make while pulsing the circle
      options - Whatever freaking particle options you want to use

    • hideEntityFromPlayer

      public static void hideEntityFromPlayer(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, org.bukkit.entity.Entity entity)
      Hiding an entity from the Player
      Parameters:
      player - player to hide entity from
      entity - entity to hide from player
      See Also:
      • showEntityToPlayer(Player, Entity)

    • drawElipse

      public static void drawElipse(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, double linearscale, double horizontalscale)
      Drawing an Elipse using Particles
      Parameters:
      center - Location of the center of the ellipse
      radius - Radius of the ellipse
      points - Number of points to use for the ellipse
      particle - Particle to use for the ellipse
      linearscale - Linear Scale of the ellipse
      horizontalscale - Horizontal Scale of the ellipse

    • drawEye

      public static void drawEye(org.bukkit.Location location, int scale)
      Drawing an Eye using Particles
      Parameters:
      location - Center Location of the Eye
      scale - Scale of the Eye

    • getGroundLocation

      public static double getGroundLocation(org.bukkit.Location loc)
      Get the highest Location at a specific point
      Parameters:
      loc - Point to get the highest location at
      Returns:
      Location of the highest block at the point

    • drawCircle

      public static void drawCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draw A circle using particles
      Parameters:
      center - Center Location of the circle
      radius - Radius of the circle
      world - World to spawn the circle in
      points - Number of points to use for the circle
      particle - Particle to use for the circle
      options - Particle Options to use for the circle

    • Clover

      public static @NonNull org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack Clover()
      Creates a new ItemStack of the Clover Signa
      Returns:
      ItemStack of the Clover Signa

    • drawPlayerTintedDisplay

      public static void drawPlayerTintedDisplay(boolean isEye, double time, org.bukkit.entity.Player player, int teleportationDuration, @Nullable org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack, org.bukkit.Location endingTransformation, org.bukkit.Color tint, int scale, double offset)
      Draws a tinted display around the player with animations and optional teleportation. This method creates an `ItemDisplay` entity and applies transformations, tint coloring, scaling, and rotational animations, while managing its lifecycle.
      Parameters:
      isEye - If true, the display originates from the player's eye location; otherwise, the player's main location is used.
      time - Duration (in seconds) for how long the display should persist.
      player - The player around whom the display is created.
      teleportationDuration - Specifies the duration (in ticks) for item display teleportation.
      itemStack - The item stack to be displayed. If null, a default leather armor item is used.
      endingTransformation - The location where the display should move to at the end of its lifecycle.
      tint - The color tint to apply to the `ItemDisplay`.
      scale - The scale of the item display.
      offset - The offset value used for rotational animation.

    • drawTintedDisplay

      public static void drawTintedDisplay(double time, org.bukkit.Location beginningTransform, int teleportationDuration, @Nullable org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack itemStack, org.bukkit.Location endingTransformation, org.bukkit.Color tint, int scale)
      Creates and manages a tinted item display entity that transitions between two locations over a configurable duration, with scaling and timing options. The display appears with a specified color tint and is automatically removed after a set time.
      Parameters:
      time - The time in seconds after which the display entity will be removed.
      beginningTransform - The starting location where the display entity will spawn.
      teleportationDuration - The duration in ticks for the display to transition between the beginning and ending transformations, clamped between 0 and 59.
      itemStack - The desired ItemStack to be displayed. If null, a default Material.LEATHER_HORSE_ARMOR with applied tint is used.
      endingTransformation - The location where the display entity will be teleported to.
      tint - The Color to apply as a tint to the displayed item.
      scale - The scaling factor to be applied to the displayed item during transformation.

    • drawCircle

      public static void drawCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle)
      Drawing a Circle with any Particle Similar to
      Parameters:
      center - Location of the center of the circle
      radius - Radius of the circle
      world - World to spawn the circle in
      points - Number of points to use for the circle
      particle - Particle to use for the circle
      See Also:
      • Only difference is that it works with literally any particle while that is meant for dust

    • secondsToTicks

      public static double secondsToTicks(double seconds)
      Converts seconds to ticks
      Parameters:
      seconds - to convert to ticks
      Returns:
      Ticks equivalent to the seconds

    • getUI

      public static org.bukkit.scoreboard.Objective getUI(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Retrieves or initializes a scoreboard objective named "UI" for the specified player. The objective displays player-specific data such as "Lives" and "SoulPoints" in the sidebar slot. If the objective does not already exist, it will be created with default settings and styled components.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose scoreboard will be retrieved or modified
      Returns:
      the initialized or existing objective named "UI" associated with the player's scoreboard

    • open

      public static void open(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, int soulvalue, int lives)
      Updates and displays a custom scoreboard for the given player based on various parameters such as soul value, lives, and cooldowns of abilities. It also handles the player's combat status.
      Parameters:
      player - The player for whom the scoreboard is being updated.
      soulvalue - The current soul value of the player.
      lives - The current number of lives the player has.

    • clearPlayerCache

      public static void clearPlayerCache(UUID playerUUID)
      Clears the souls HashMap along with Ability Cooldowns for one Player
      Parameters:
      playerUUID - uuid of player you are removing

    • loadingBar

      public static net.kyori.adventure.text.Component loadingBar(double value, double max, double min)
      Generates a loading bar representation as a Component based on the provided value, maximum, and minimum limits. The loading bar is displayed using a fixed number of segments, where filled segments represent the progress proportionally, and unfilled segments represent the remaining portion.
      Parameters:
      value - The current progress value to be represented by the loading bar.
      max - The maximum limit of the progress range. Must be greater than the minimum limit.
      min - The minimum limit of the progress range. Must be less than the maximum limit.
      Returns:
      A Component object containing the styled loading bar representation, or a default fallback representation if the maximum limit is less than or equal to the minimum limit.

    • createMinMagicCircleAroundPlayer

      public static void createMinMagicCircleAroundPlayer(org.bukkit.entity.Player target, int tickRate)
      Creates a minimal magic circle effect around the specified player. The circle's color is determined by the player's soul type, and it evolves over time with visual effects.
      Parameters:
      target - The player around whom the magic circle will be generated.
      tickRate - The rate at which the magic circle effect updates, measured in ticks.

    • createMinMagicCircle

      public static void createMinMagicCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, int tickRate, SoulTypes soulType)
      Creates a magic circle animation at a given location with specific particle effects and behavior based on the soul type. This method spawns particle effects in patterns, which dynamically evolve over time. The animation stops under certain conditions such as exceeding a threshold tick count or changes in the block state at the specified location.
      Parameters:
      center - The central Location where the magic circle will be created.
      tickRate - The rate at which the animation progresses, affecting how frequently particles are updated.
      soulType - The type of SoulTypes that determines the color of the particles used in the animation.

    • createMinMagicCircle

      public static void createMinMagicCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, int tickRate, SoulTypes soulType, int time)
      Creates a minimal magic circle visual effect at a specified location. The effect's color and behavior are determined by the specified soul type and timing parameters.
      Parameters:
      center - The Location where the magic circle will be created.
      tickRate - The rate at which the effects update, in game ticks.
      soulType - The type of soul determining the color of the magic circle. Available soul types include: Green, BaseGreen, Red, BaseRed, Cafe, BaseCafe, Orange, BaseOrange, BaseMocha, Mocha, Black, BaseBlack.
      time - The duration (in game ticks) for which the effect should last if the location is not a lectern or the item on a lectern becomes invalid.

    • createVerticleMinMagicCircle

      public static void createVerticleMinMagicCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, int tickRate, SoulTypes soulType, double yaw, org.bukkit.Location pivot, int time, double scale)
      Creates a vertical magic circle animation with a specified color and properties. The animation's appearance and behavior depend on the provided soul type, rotation, scale, and time duration.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location where the magic circle is created.
      tickRate - The rate at which the circle animation updates.
      soulType - The type of soul that determines the color of the magic circle.
      yaw - The yaw rotation angle to apply, influencing the orientation of the circle.
      pivot - The pivot point used for rotation calculations.
      time - The total duration (in ticks) for which the magic circle should animate.
      scale - The scaling factor to modify the size of the magic circle.

    • createMaxMagicCircle

      public static void createMaxMagicCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, int tickRate, int scale, SoulTypes soulType)
      Creates a "magic circle" effect at a given location using particles, tick rate, scaling, and soul type. This method animates the circle with specific patterns and colors, dynamically determined based on the soul type. The task automatically cancels based on certain conditions, such as the absence of a lectern or an empty inventory slot.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location where the magic circle will be created.
      tickRate - The rate at which the animation progresses, controlling the speed of updates.
      scale - The scale or size of the magic circle, influencing its dimensions and effect radius.
      soulType - The type of soul that determines the color of the magic circle and its thematic representation.

    • createRemoteExplosionParticles

      public static void createRemoteExplosionParticles(org.bukkit.Location center, int tickrate, int size)
      Creates and animates remote explosion particles at a specified location. The animation includes rotational effects, particle trails, sound effects, and ends with a simulated explosion.
      Parameters:
      center - The central Location where the explosion particles originate.
      tickrate - The rate at which the particle rotation and animation steps occur, measured in ticks.
      size - The power or scale of the explosion, determining the size and intensity of the particle effects.

    • createMaxMagicCircleAroundPlayer

      public void createMaxMagicCircleAroundPlayer(org.bukkit.entity.Player target, int tickRate, int scale)
      Creates a maximum magic circle effect around the specified player. The visual effect is dynamic and involves particles, changing intention and color based on the player's soul
      Parameters:
      tickRate - The rate at which the animation progresses, affecting the speed of the effect.
      scale - The scale of the magic circle, affecting its size and visual impact.

    • randompointoncircle

      public static void randompointoncircle(org.bukkit.Location center, int points, int radius, org.bukkit.Particle particle)
      Gets a random point on a circle
      Parameters:
      center - of the center of the circle
      points - How many points on the original Circle
      radius - The Radius of the Original Circle
      particle - Particle Type that you want to spawn as the little pillar thing

    • broadcast

      public void broadcast(String message)
      Broadcast a message to all online players
      Parameters:
      message - The message you want to broadcast

    • multisquare

      public static void multisquare(org.bukkit.Location location, int tick, int size, org.bukkit.Particle particle, int initialrot, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Creates a multilayered square looking Just like a magic circle
      Parameters:
      location - Center Location of the Magic Circle
      tick - Tick offset you want to start at
      size - size of the multisquare, acts as a scale
      particle - Particle Type that you want to spawn as the little pillar thing
      initialrot - Initial offset of the circle's rotations
      options - Any Dust Options you want to use

    • Vertmultisquare

      public static void Vertmultisquare(org.bukkit.Location location, int tick, double size, org.bukkit.Particle particle, int initialrot, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, double yaw)
      Creates a verticle multisquare similar to multisquare
      Parameters:
      location - Center Location of the Magic Circle
      tick - Tick offset you want to start at
      size - size of the multisquare, acts as a scale
      particle - Particle Type that you want to spawn as the little pillar thing
      initialrot - Initial offset of the circle's rotations
      options - Any Dust Options you want to use
      yaw - Yaw angle for the vertical square

    • drawSquare

      public static void drawSquare(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, double rot, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit)
      Draws a square shape using particles at a specified location, with configurable size, rotation, particle type, and dust options.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location of the square.
      size - The size of the square, affecting its width and height.
      rot - The initial rotation angle of the square.
      particle - Particle type to spawn for the square.
      options - Dust options to apply to the particles in the square.
      xlimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the X axis
      ylimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the Y axis
      zlimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the Z axis

    • drawVerticleSquare

      public static void drawVerticleSquare(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, double rot, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit)
      Draws a verticle square shape using particles at a specified location, with configurable size, rotation, particle type, and dust options.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location of the square.
      size - The size of the square, affecting its width and height.
      rot - The initial rotation angle of the square.
      particle - Particle type to spawn for the square.
      options - Dust options to apply to the particles in the square.
      xlimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the X axis
      ylimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the Y axis
      zlimit - A cutting variable that stops the drawing at a certain point, to allow for "loading animations" on the Z axis

    • drawVerticleSquare

      public static void drawVerticleSquare(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, double rot, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit, boolean baller, double yaw)
      Draws a vertical square using particles in a 3D space, with options for size, rotation, and positional limits.
      Parameters:
      center - The central Location around which the square will be drawn.
      size - The length of the sides of the square. This value will be halved internally to determine the distance from the center.
      rot - The rotation angle in radians to apply to the square around the Y-axis.
      particle - The Particle type used to draw the square.
      options - Additional DustOptions for customizing particle appearance, such as color and size.
      xlimit - The limit on the X-axis beyond which particles will not be rendered.
      ylimit - The limit on the Y-axis beyond which particles will not be rendered.
      zlimit - The limit on the Z-axis beyond which particles will not be rendered.
      baller - A boolean flag determining whether specific rotational effects will be applied during rendering.
      yaw - The yaw angle in degrees to apply when rotating the square along its Z-axis.

    • rotpointX

      public static org.bukkit.Location rotpointX(org.bukkit.Location pivot, double angleDegrees, org.bukkit.Location toRotate)
      Rotates a point around a pivot on the XZ-plane by a specified angle in degrees.
      Parameters:
      pivot - the location used as the center of rotation
      angleDegrees - the angle in degrees to rotate the point
      toRotate - the location of the point to be rotated
      Returns:
      the new location of the point after rotation

    • drawClock

      public static void drawClock(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, int circlePoints, int handPoints, int hours, int minutes, org.bukkit.Particle faceParticle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions faceOptions, org.bukkit.Particle tickParticle, org.bukkit.Particle handParticle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions minuteOptions, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions hourOptions)
      Draws a clock visualization at the specified location using particle effects. The clock includes a face, tick marks, an hour hand, and a minute hand. The appearance of the clock components is customizable through various particle options.
      Parameters:
      center - the location where the clock will be drawn
      radius - the radius of the clock face
      circlePoints - the number of points used to render the circular clock face
      handPoints - the number of points used to render the clock hands
      hours - the hour value of the time to display on the clock (0-23)
      minutes - the minute value of the time to display on the clock (0-59)
      faceParticle - the type of particle to use for the clock face
      faceOptions - additional customization options for the clock face particle
      tickParticle - the type of particle to use for the tick marks
      handParticle - the type of particle to use for the hour and minute hands
      minuteOptions - additional customization options for the minute hand particle
      hourOptions - additional customization options for the hour hand particle

    • drawClocklines

      public static void drawClocklines(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, double rot)
      Draws clock-like lines radiating outward from a central point on a circular plane.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location around which the clocklines are drawn.
      radius - The radius of the circle for the clocklines.
      world - The world in which the particles will be displayed.
      points - The total number of points (or divisions) around the circle.
      particle - The particle type to be displayed for the clocklines.
      options - The particle dust options, if applicable for the given particle type.
      rot - The rotational offset in radians for the starting angle of the clocklines.

    • rotpointZ

      public static org.bukkit.Location rotpointZ(org.bukkit.Location pivot, double angleDegrees, org.bukkit.Location toRotate)
      Rotates a 3D point around the Z-axis by a specified angle relative to a pivot point.
      Parameters:
      pivot - The pivot point around which the rotation is performed.
      angleDegrees - The rotation angle in degrees.
      toRotate - The point to rotate.
      Returns:
      A new Location representing the rotated point.

    • rotpointY

      public static org.bukkit.Location rotpointY(org.bukkit.Location pivot, double angleDegrees, org.bukkit.Location toRotate)
      Rotates a point around the Y-axis relative to a specified pivot point by a given angle.
      Parameters:
      pivot - the location of the pivot point around which the rotation is performed
      angleDegrees - the angle of rotation in degrees, measured clockwise
      toRotate - the location of the point to be rotated
      Returns:
      the new location of the point after being rotated around the Y-axis

    • drawParticleLine

      public static void drawParticleLine(org.bukkit.Location start, org.bukkit.Location end, org.bukkit.World world, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions optionsdouble, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit)
      Draws a line of particles between two locations in the specified world. The particle line can have limitations in the x, y, and z directions, and supports custom particle options for dynamic effects.
      Parameters:
      start - The starting location of the particle line.
      end - The ending location of the particle line.
      world - The world in which the particles will be displayed.
      particle - The type of particle to be used for the line.
      optionsdouble - The additional options for the DUST particle effect, used when the particle type is Particle.DUST.
      xlimit - The distance along the x-axis after which particles will start being drawn.
      ylimit - The distance along the y-axis that affects particle display (currently unused in logic).
      zlimit - The distance along the z-axis that affects particle display (currently unused in logic).

    • PortalParticleLifespan

      public static void PortalParticleLifespan(org.bukkit.Location center, org.bukkit.Location targetLocation)
      Creates and manages the lifecycle of a particle-based portal effect at the specified location. The portal progresses through three states: Opening, Open, and Closing, with particles displayed to depict these transitions. Additionally, players near the portal during its "Open" state are teleported to the specified target location.
      Parameters:
      center - The location where the portal is centered and particles are displayed.
      targetLocation - The location to which players will be teleported when near the portal during its "Open" state.

    • PortalParticleLifespan

      public static void PortalParticleLifespan(org.bukkit.Location center, org.bukkit.Location targetLocation, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Creates a particle animation to simulate a portal with states: opening, open, and closing. While the portal is open, nearby players are teleported to the target location. The portal animation progresses over time and eventually stops after a set duration.
      Parameters:
      center - The location at which the portal particles are created. This is the center of the portal.
      targetLocation - The destination to which players will be teleported when they interact with the portal.
      options - The particle options used to define the appearance of the portal particles.

    • createPortalParticles

      public static void createPortalParticles(org.bukkit.Location center, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit)
      Creates portal particle effects at the specified location. The particle shapes can either form a circle or multiple vertical squares depending on the configuration.
      Parameters:
      center - The central Location where the particles will be generated.
      xlimit - The x-axis limit controlling the particle effect spread in the x-direction.
      ylimit - The y-axis limit controlling the particle effect spread in the y-direction and affecting particle size.
      zlimit - The z-axis limit controlling the particle effect spread in the z-direction.

    • createPortalParticles

      public static void createPortalParticles(org.bukkit.Location center, double xlimit, double ylimit, double zlimit, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions ring, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions inside)
      Creates portal particle effects around a given location by generating various patterns such as circles or squares. The type, color, and size of the particles can be customized using the provided parameters.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location where the portal particles will be created.
      xlimit - The horizontal limit defining the size or spread of the particle patterns along the X-axis.
      ylimit - The vertical limit defining the size or spread of the particle patterns along the Y-axis.
      zlimit - The depth limit defining the size or spread of the particle patterns along the Z-axis.
      ring - The `Particle.DustOptions` object specifying the properties (e.g., color, size) of the outer-ring particles.
      inside - The `Particle.DustOptions` object specifying the properties (e.g., color, size) of the inner particles.

    • drawverticleCircle

      public static void drawverticleCircle(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a vertical circle in the specified world using particles.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location of the circle.
      radius - The radius of the circle.
      world - The world where the circle will be drawn.
      points - The number of points (or particles) to use for forming the circle.
      particle - The type of particle to use for drawing the circle.
      options - The particle options to be applied if the particle type is Particle.DUST.

    • getSkull

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack getSkull(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Creates and returns an ItemStack representing a player head with the specified player's skin.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose skin will be applied to the player head.
      Returns:
      An ItemStack representing a player head with the specified player's skin.

    • getCustomSkull

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack getCustomSkull(String base64)
      Creates a custom player head item with a texture based on the provided Base64-encoded string.
      Parameters:
      base64 - the Base64-encoded texture string to apply to the player head. If the string is empty, a plain player head is returned.
      Returns:
      an ItemStack representing the custom player head with the specified texture.

    • getPlayerSkin

      public static net.skinsrestorer.api.property.SkinProperty getPlayerSkin(org.bukkit.entity.Player player) throws net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException
      Retrieves the skin property of a specified player.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose skin property is to be retrieved
      Returns:
      the skin property of the player, or null if not found
      Throws:
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException - if there is an error during the skin data retrieval process

    • getSoulType

      public static SoulTypes getSoulType(org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Determines the soul type of a given player.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose soul type is to be determined. The player must have their soul type stored in the persistent data container.
      Returns:
      The determined soul type of the player. If the player does not have a soul type stored, it defaults to SoulTypes.Red and opens a selection UI.

    • setSkinByName

      public static void setSkinByName(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, String skinName) throws net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException, net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException
      Sets the skin of a player based on the specified skin name.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose skin is to be updated.
      skinName - The name of the skin to apply to the player.
      Throws:
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException - If an error occurs while processing the skin data.
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException - If an error occurs while requesting skin data.

    • setSkinByUrl

      public static void setSkinByUrl(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, String url) throws net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException, net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException
      Sets the skin of a given player using a skin image retrieved from a specified URL.
      Parameters:
      player - The player whose skin is to be set.
      url - The URL of the skin image to be applied. Must be a valid image link.
      Throws:
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.MineSkinException - If an error occurs while generating the skin using the MineSkin API.
      net.skinsrestorer.api.exception.DataRequestException - If there is an issue during the data request to fetch the skin information.

    • setSkinByProperties

      public static void setSkinByProperties(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, String value, String signature)
      Sets the skin of the specified player using the provided skin properties.
      Parameters:
      player - the player whose skin is to be updated
      value - the value of the skin property
      signature - the signature of the skin property

    • drawSphere

      public static void drawSphere(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a sphere by spawning particles at locations calculated around the given center point.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location of the sphere.
      radius - The radius of the sphere.
      points - The number of points to use for each dimension in rendering the sphere.
      particle - The type of particle to use for the sphere.
      options - The options for the particle if the particle type is Particle.DUST, otherwise ignored.

    • drawLine

      public static void drawLine(org.bukkit.Location start, org.bukkit.Location end, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a line in the world by spawning particles between two given locations.
      Parameters:
      start - the starting location of the line
      end - the ending location of the line
      world - the world in which the line will be drawn
      points - the number of points (particles) to spawn along the line
      particle - the type of particle to use for drawing the line
      options - additional options for customizing the appearance of the particles

    • drawLine

      public static void drawLine(org.bukkit.Location start, org.bukkit.Location end, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, int offsetX, int offsetY, int offsetZ, int extra, org.bukkit.Color color)
      Draws a line between two locations in a specified world using particles. QUICK DISCLAIMER, THIS IS DIFFERENT THAN OTHER DRAW LINE BECAUSE THIS IS MEANT FOR GENERAL PARTICLES
      Parameters:
      start - The starting location of the line.
      end - The ending location of the line.
      world - The world in which the particles will be displayed.
      points - The number of points (or segments) along the line where particles will be spawned.
      particle - The type of particle to display along the line.
      offsetX - The offset for the particle's movement along the X-axis.
      offsetY - The offset for the particle's movement along the Y-axis.
      offsetZ - The offset for the particle's movement along the Z-axis.
      extra - Additional data or modifiers for the particle, such as speed or size, depending on the particle type.
      color - The color of the particle, if the particle type supports coloring.

    • drawLine

      public static void drawLine(org.bukkit.Location start, org.bukkit.Location end, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle)
      Draws a line between two locations in the specified world using particles.
      Parameters:
      start - The starting location of the line.
      end - The ending location of the line.
      world - The world in which the line will be drawn.
      points - The number of points to distribute along the line, determining its density.
      particle - The type of particle to use for drawing the line.

    • drawIsoscelesTriangle

      public static void drawIsoscelesTriangle(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws an isosceles triangle using particles in the specified world.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location of the triangle's base.
      size - The size of the triangle. This determines the distance from the center to the vertices.
      world - The world in which the triangle will be drawn.
      points - The number of points to use for drawing the edges of the triangle.
      particle - The type of particle to use for drawing the triangle.
      options - Additional options for the particle appearance, such as color (if applicable).

    • drawIsoscelesTriangle

      public static void drawIsoscelesTriangle(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, org.bukkit.Location offset)
      Draws an isosceles triangle in the given world using particles, based on the specified parameters.
      Parameters:
      center - the central location of the triangle, which determines its position
      size - the size of the triangle, affecting the distance from the center to each vertex
      world - the world in which the triangle will be drawn
      points - the number of points to be used for rendering the lines of the triangle
      particle - the type of particle to be used for drawing the triangle
      options - particle rendering options to be applied, such as color or size
      offset - an offset applied to the triangle's vertices to adjust its position

    • drawSpiral

      public static void drawSpiral(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, int turns, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a spiral originating from a specified center location in the given world. The spiral expands outward based on the specified radius and completes a number of turns, with a configurable number of points and visual appearance.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location from which the spiral originates.
      radius - The maximum radius of the spiral at its furthest point from the center.
      turns - The number of complete turns the spiral will make.
      world - The world in which the spiral will be drawn.
      points - The number of particle points used to draw the spiral, affecting its smoothness.
      particle - The type of particle used to render the spiral.
      options - Additional options for customizing the appearance of the particle.

    • drawStar

      public static void drawStar(org.bukkit.Location center, double radius, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a star-shaped pattern in the world using particles.
      Parameters:
      center - The center location of the star.
      radius - The radius of the outer points of the star.
      world - The world in which the star will be drawn.
      points - The number of particles to draw between each point of the star.
      particle - The particle type to use for drawing the star.
      options - The dust options for the particle, used if the particle type supports it.

    • Lift

      public static void Lift(org.bukkit.entity.Player target)
      Elevates the target player and performs visual effects based on their soul type. This method creates a star effect with alternating colors around the target player for a duration of 10 ticks.
      Parameters:
      target - The player to be affected and elevated. This method uses the player's current location and persistent data to determine the behavior and effects.

    • drawExclamationPoint

      public static void drawExclamationPoint(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, org.bukkit.Location offset)
      Draws an exclamation point at the specified location using particles.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location where the exclamation point will be drawn.
      size - The size of the exclamation point. This determines the length of the shaft and the size of the circle.
      points - The number of points used to draw the lines and circle, affecting the visual smoothness.
      particle - The particle type used to render the exclamation point.
      options - Additional options for customizing the appearance of the particle (e.g., color or transition effects).
      offset - The offset to apply to the particle positions during drawing.

    • drawDangerSymbol

      public static void drawDangerSymbol(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions outside, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions inside)
      Draws a danger symbol composed of an exclamation point and an enclosing isosceles triangle.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location where the danger symbol will be drawn.
      size - The size factor for scaling the danger symbol.
      points - The resolution or number of points for rendering the shapes.
      particle - The type of particle to use for drawing the symbol.
      outside - The visual options for the particles used in the outer triangle.
      inside - The visual options for the particles used in the exclamation point.

    • drawHeart

      public static void drawHeart(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options)
      Draws a heart shape in the world using the specified particle effect.
      Parameters:
      center - The central location of the heart shape.
      size - The size multiplier for the heart shape.
      world - The world in which the particles will be displayed.
      points - The number of points used to define the heart's shape. A higher value will result in smoother curves.
      particle - The particle effect to use for drawing the heart shape.
      options - The dust options to customize the appearance of the particles, applicable if the particle type supports it.

    • getNearbyTrusted

      public static List<org.bukkit.entity.Player> getNearbyTrusted(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, int radius)
      Retrieves a list of nearby trusted players within a specified radius of a given player. A player is considered trusted if both players mutually trust each other.
      Parameters:
      player - the player for whom nearby trusted players are to be retrieved
      radius - the radius within which to search for other trusted players
      Returns:
      a list of players who are within the specified radius and are mutually trusted by the given player

    • getSmeltingResult

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack getSmeltingResult(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack item)
      Retrieves the smelting result of the given item stack based on furnace recipes.
      Parameters:
      item - The ItemStack to check for a smelting result. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      The ItemStack that represents the smelted result, with the same amount as the input item. Returns null if no matching furnace recipe is found.

    • isSmeltable

      public static boolean isSmeltable(org.bukkit.Material material)
      Checks if the given material can be smelted using a furnace recipe.
      Parameters:
      material - the material to check for smelting compatibility
      Returns:
      true if the material can be smelted, false otherwise

    • silenceFor

      public static void silenceFor(org.bukkit.entity.Player player, int seconds)
      Applies a temporary silence effect to a player for the specified number of seconds. During the silence period, the silence state is tracked and removed automatically after the duration expires.
      Parameters:
      player - The player to whom the silence effect will be applied.
      seconds - The duration of the silence effect in seconds.

    • isItemNull

      public static boolean isItemNull(org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack stack)
      Checks if the given ItemStack is null or represents air.
      Parameters:
      stack - the ItemStack to be checked
      Returns:
      true if the ItemStack is null or its type is Material.AIR, false otherwise

    • getEnchantment

      @NotNull public static @NotNull org.bukkit.enchantments.Enchantment getEnchantment(@NotNull @Value @NotNull String key)
      Retrieves an Enchantment object associated with the specified key.
      Parameters:
      key - the unique string key representing the enchantment within the Minecraft namespace. This parameter must not be null.
      Returns:
      the Enchantment corresponding to the given key, never null.

    • constructColoredBottle

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack constructColoredBottle(List<org.bukkit.NamespacedKey> keys, List<String> values, org.bukkit.Color color)
      Constructs a colored potion bottle with the provided keys and values stored as persistent data.
      Parameters:
      keys - A list of NamespacedKeys to store in the potion's persistent data container.
      values - A list of strings corresponding to the keys to be stored as persistent data.
      color - The color of the potion.
      Returns:
      An ItemStack representing the colored potion bottle with the specified persistent data.

    • dealTrueDamage

      public static void dealTrueDamage(org.bukkit.entity.LivingEntity entity, double amount)
      Deals true damage to a living entity, bypassing armor and resistances. If the entity's health drops to or below zero, it will be killed and the standard death event will be triggered.
      Parameters:
      entity - The living entity to deal true damage to. If the entity is already dead, no action is taken.
      amount - The amount of true damage to deal to the entity. This damage directly subtracts from the entity's health.

    • Fireball

      public static org.bukkit.inventory.ItemStack Fireball()
      Creates and returns an ItemStack representing a fireball. The item uses the material FIRE_CHARGE and assigns a custom item model associated with the "fireball" NamespacedKey.
      Returns:
      an ItemStack configured as a fireball with a specific item model.

    • initFireball

      public static org.bukkit.entity.ItemDisplay initFireball(org.bukkit.entity.Entity fireball, org.bukkit.entity.Player player)
      Initializes a fireball as an ItemDisplay entity, spawns it in the player's world with the appropriate location and orientation, and sets its item stack to a fireball.
      Parameters:
      fireball - The entity to initialize. This is replaced with the newly spawned ItemDisplay entity.
      player - The player whose location and direction are used to position and orient the fireball.
      Returns:
      The initialized ItemDisplay entity representing the fireball.

    • drawArrow

      public static void drawArrow(org.bukkit.Location center, double size, org.bukkit.World world, int points, org.bukkit.Particle particle, org.bukkit.Particle.DustOptions options, float rot)
      Draws an arrow in the specified world using particles. The arrow is composed of an isosceles triangle representing the arrowhead and a line representing the shaft.
      Parameters:
      center - The location representing the center of the arrow.
      size - The size of the arrow, determining the length and dimensions.
      world - The world where the arrow will be drawn.
      points - The number of points used to draw the arrow's shapes (affects smoothness).
      particle - The particle type used to render the arrow.
      options - The particle options for customization, such as color or size.
      rot - The rotation of the arrow in degrees, applied about the center.
